← All projects Case study — P.03 · Consumer · Marketplace

Eternally

Most families no longer live near their ancestral cemeteries. Visiting requires travel; maintaining graves requires proximity; sharing memories requires coordination. Eternally makes all three possible from anywhere.

Role Founder & Engineer Status Live Scale 10,000+ memorials

What it does

Life-history timeline

A collaborative storytelling tool where family members contribute memories, milestones, and photos to build a comprehensive biography — the full arc of a person’s life, not just their dates.

AI-enhanced photo & video gallery

Decades-old photos are automatically sharpened, colorized, and restored through Cloudinary AI pipelines. The gallery becomes a permanent, high-quality visual record.

Interactive cemetery map

A digital map with offline access — visitors navigate to the exact gravesite without searching, and cemetery operators can embed it for wayfinding.

Remote grave services marketplace

Families order flowers, professional cleaning, seasonal decorations, and memorial items — delivered and placed by local providers. A family in California can have flowers placed on a grave in Hawaii for a birthday or anniversary.

Moderated guest book & privacy controls

Friends and community members leave memories in a curated guest book — unlike social comments, the family controls what becomes part of the permanent memorial, with public, family-only, or unlisted visibility.

Why I built it

The U.S. death care industry generates $23B+ annually, yet cemetery operators still run on paper records and phone calls. This is a classic picks-and-shovels opportunity: don’t compete with funeral homes — give cemetery operators the digital infrastructure they lack and charge for the platform.

Legacy industries with fragmented operators and no digital incumbents are exactly where platform businesses get built.

The remote services marketplace is the wedge. Flowers, cleaning, and decorations create recurring revenue that operators can white-label. Once you own the digital relationship between family and cemetery, you own the distribution channel for every adjacent service: estate planning referrals, memorial events, genealogy integrations.
